findings.finding: Capacity-wait reconciliation runs on every poll and releases provider plus local repository reservations for missing, terminal, inactive, or unroutable waiting issues; a release failure retains local fencing for safe retry.
A refreshed claim owned by another run drops only the stale local reservation and does not heartbeat or release the foreign provider claim.
A valid same-run waiting claim is refreshed and heartbeated without consuming a new Attempt or retry budget; regression coverage asserts attempt 3 and an unchanged empty retry-attempt map.

Latest durable evidence confirms a clean local HEAD at 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36; an independent live GitHub comparison confirms bos/issue-51 is identical to that SHA. Exact-commit evidence reports 363 tests passing, 100.00% coverage, strict Credo, Dialyzer, formatting, and diff hygiene passing.

summary: Passed functional review at exact commit 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36. The implementation and regression evidence affirm all pre-merge capacity-wait acceptance behavior, with local and remote identity bound to the reviewed SHA; merged-artifact installation and service health remain the defined post-merge verification.
